theme_notes: Zverev finally won his first Grand Slam at Roland-Garros after 28 years of near-misses; NBA Finals Game 3 tonight with the Knicks up 2-0 on the Spurs; Pope Leo XIV (the first American pope) addressed the Spanish parliament during his Madrid visit; a 7.8 earthquake struck the Philippines killing 32; Israel and Iran exchanged missiles before a fragile ceasefire.
settled: Even. Pope Leo XIV didn't cross an ocean to watch you freeload.
Zero balance. Zverev finally got what he deserved. Turns out you can too.
Clean slate. The Knicks know how long these can take. Don't be the Knicks.
youOwe: Under fifty. The Spurs are also down 0-2. You're both having a rough June.
Less than a courtside seat. More than you're comfortable admitting.
Pocket change. Pope Leo's in Spain calling for moral renewal. He's talking to you.
A hundred dollars. Zverev waited 28 years. They'll survive two more weeks.
Enough for a Roland-Garros souvenir and one bad airport crêpe. You chose this.
Buck fifty. Zverev won a Slam. You can't settle a tab. Different trajectories.
Three hundred dollars. The American pope is in Madrid giving speeches. You're giving excuses.
Four hundred. Zverev sobbed on his back in the clay. That's you, but financially.
The Philippines had a 7.8 earthquake this week. Your finances are registering similarly.
Eight hundred. That's Knicks Finals tickets if you stand in a stairwell.
Near a grand. Israel and Iran escalated over smaller differences.
That's Zverev's therapy budget. He used it and finally won something. Think.
Four figures. The Ebola outbreak spreads at unprecedented pace. Same energy as this tab.
Two grand. You're not a partner. You're a geopolitical liability.
Enough to make Zverev cry again. He just stopped. Don't set him off.
Five grand. Pope Leo addressed a parliament about dignity. You have none left.
More dollars owed than the Knicks have gone without a title. Historical.
This is a natural disaster. Not in the Philippines sense. In every other sense.
theyOwe: Forty bucks owed to you. Zverev waited 28 years for his. You have the moral high ground.
Under fifty. The Knicks are up 2-0. You can afford confidence.
Small sum. Still on the books. Pope Leo would approve of the accountability.
A hundred bucks. You're in better shape than the Spurs. Collect.
Enough for Game 3 nosebleeds. They owe you the money and a ticket.
They'll pay you back. The Knicks waited 53 years and are still standing. So are you.
Three hundred. You've been patient with a 7.8-level imbalance. Time to shake.
Four hundred bucks. You've been Zverev-patient. Collect like you've already won.
Enough to buy a Roland-Garros keepsake. Take the trophy. Actually, take the cash.
Eight hundred. Israel and Iran reached a ceasefire faster than you'll see this.
Almost a thousand owed. The Knicks never waited this long for their money. Neither should you.
Getting into formal-document territory. Zverev got a trophy. You want a wire transfer.
Four figures owed. The Spurs would trade anything to be in your position right now.
Two thousand dollars. You're the Knicks — up, in control. Don't blow it.
They owe you enough to make Pope Leo reschedule an appearance. Collect with that energy.
Five grand. Zverev won the Open. You won leverage. Use it.
You're owed more than the Knicks' 53-year drought in years. That's not nothing.
This is geopolitical. Israel and Iran have fought over less.
